pr_description:
Bumps up our `mobx` and `mobx-react` dependencies to 6.x.y. This is part of a wider attempt to modernize our renderer codebase, since we're stuck on old versions of React and using class components.

### Codemod

In order to perform this upgrade, I followed MobX's [Migrating from MobX 4/5](https://mobx.js.org/migrating-from-4-or-5.html) guide.

Because we use `@observer` decorators extensively, I decided to use the [`npx mobx-undecorate` codemod](https://www.npmjs.com/package/mobx-undecorate) provided by the guide. This will allow us to immediately upgrade to MobX 6 while keeping our React component architecture.

We can then perform a piecewise upgrade of our React components in the future.

### Caveats

* The types for the new `observer()` higher-order component from MobX mean that we use `any` types when mounting Enzyme components. This is a trade-off I'm willing to make because Enzyme doesn't support React 17 yet and we might want to look towards migrating towards another unit testing library.
* This diff is huge. Reviewing it will be easier with the "hide whitespace" option.
commit_message: chore: update to mobx@6 and apply codemod

Replaces the old `commands-bisect-spec.tsx` Enzyme test with new shiny RTL ones.

Along the way, there are a couple non-test changes to note:
* New dependency: `@testing-library/user-event` is apparently the way to go to simulate user events over the built-in `fireEvent` helper method.
* I added new `aria-label` properties to the 👍 and 👎 buttons. This makes the buttons more accessible!

https://github.com/electron/fiddle/issues/1176

This PR adds a checkbox to the Github settings to allow users to select whether they want to publish their gist as a revision of the default Fiddle startup editor values. This is useful when users submit issues, with Fiddle gists as repro examples: this change will allow maintainers to quickly spot diffs easier.


✅  Added option in settings to include checkbox
✅    Added tests to expand coverage (wip)
✅  Validated on Mac, (needs to be tested on Windows and Linux)

![ezgif com-gif-maker (1)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33054982/191142738-4d42e530-008e-406f-9b91-3d792663aef0.gif)

Another step towards #1408. I've added a couple of changes on top of just migrating the tests to RTL (open to feedback if any of them are a bad idea):

- extracted some duplicate version mocking logic to a separate file
- created a helper function to render the `VersionSelect` component - I didn't do it in a `beforeEach` block because we only need to render the whole component in one test
- added a missing test for the `renderVersionContextMenu` component

~~I've also included the `testMatch` change from #1434 just so the RTL tests will run on CI for this PR. This means that #1434 should land first to keep things separate and to prevent it from becoming an empty changeset.~~
commit_message: test: use RTL in the version selector tests

pr_description:
Adresses #1408. This should be easier to review one commit at a time, as Github doesn't seem to be picking up the move across folders.

I removed the window resizing tests from `tour.spec.tsx` as it's not possible to access the component state in RTL (it's an implementation detail) and I didn't figure out a way to test that using something a user could see and interact with.

pr_description:
Ref: #1408.

I'm also adding a helper function that allows us to access class components' instances, since RTL doesn't expose them and a bunch of our tests are messing with component internals directly; that's probably something we should move away from at some point, but for now that helper should make it a bit easier to migrate away from Enzyme.

Ref: #1408.

I'm also adding a helper function that allows us to access class components' instances, since RTL doesn't expose them and a bunch of our tests are messing with component internals directly; that's probably something we should move away from at some point, but for now that helper should make it a bit easier to migrate away from Enzyme.

Ref: #1408.

This is easier to review one commit at a time since Github is not picking up the move across folders.

I've refactored `renderNonIdealState` so that we render it as JSX (`<RenderNonIdealState editorMosaic={editorMosaic} />`) instead of calling it as a regular function (`renderNonIdealState(editorMosaic)`) like we were doing.

Ref: #1408.

This is easier to review one commit at a time since Github is not picking up the move across folders.

I've refactored `renderNonIdealState` so that we render it as JSX (`<RenderNonIdealState editorMosaic={editorMosaic} />`) instead of calling it as a regular function (`renderNonIdealState(editorMosaic)`) like we were doing.
Prior to this the table loaded all possible electron versions with no real ability to filter or search. This PR virtualizes the list and adds input filtering.

Stacks on top of #1717, and prerequisite to bumping `@electron/fiddle-core` since I don't want to mess with the rat's nest which is Jest + ESM.

The majority of these line changes are mechanical `jest` -> `vi`, but there are some non-trivial bits which I'll comment on inline.

The changes from `import * as fs from 'fs-extra'` to `import fs from 'fs-extra'` are to make Vitest happy - there's no functional impact.

Closes #215.

Adds a `react-mosaic-component` wrapper around the `Editors` and the `Output` components so that you can drag and resize.

Due to this resizing fix, the functionality of the console show/hide button had to be refactored as well. Note that pressing show/hide now does not have an animation. Moreover, showing the hidden console will always revert to its default size.

### TODO
- [x] Rewrite close console test to pass

cc @felixrieseberg @codebytere @DeerMichel

Closes #244. Adds a version bisect helper for Electron Fiddle. Inspired by [git bisect](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-bisect), this feature helps Electron contributors or Electron users reporting a bug to pinpoint the exact version where a bug started occurring. ## Algorithm Taken directly from the Chromium bisect code: https://github.com/chromium/chromium/blob/master/tools/bisect-builds.py#L839 ## Screenshots Dialog allowing the selection of a version range. Once range is chosen, immediately jump to pivot version and allow user to continue w/ 3 options: good version/bad version/cancel. ## Bonus This PR also adds graceful edge behavior for the commands bar. It will now scroll horizontally when overflowing. ## Considerations - Is the experience intuitive? - Is code re-use between the existing Version selector and the new Bisect dialog optimized?

Fixes #269.

### Context
Our upgrade to Electron 7 inadvertently broke our usage of `<input type="file" webkitdirectory/>`, which is a non-standard browser API designed to allow the file input to select entire directories ([MDN link](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLInputElement/webkitdirectory)).

In Electron <= 6, the returned `files` object returned the selected folder itself as. This was incorrect behaviour, and was fixed in this PR: https://github.com/electron/electron/pull/18343.

Starting in Electron 7, the returned `files` object returns the correct value: a `FileList` of all files. However, we no longer have a direct reference to our selected folder.

### Change
~Instead, there's some jank path manipulation going on, where we grab an arbitrary file, and using its absolute path and relative path from the selected folder, obtain the absolute path of the selected folder.~

No more jank. PR instead leverages Electron's `dialog.showOpenDialog()` API to select the folder and retrieve its path.
commit_message: fix: replace add version dialog with electron api

pr_description:
Closes #298 

This PR builds upon the work done in #254, #292, and #293, mirroring what we did to support `preload.js` to incorporate a `styles.css` file.

(This also means that any future bugs found with `preload` probably apply to `css` as well 😂)

This feature will be helpful for app developers looking to use Fiddle as a playground for app ideas beyond merely testing API functionality.

Fixes #335 by ensuring that the last open editor cannot be closed.

This means two things happen when only one editor is open:
1) The toolbar controls on the top right of the panel (expand, close) are hidden.
2) You cannot close the last editor through the `commands-editor` component.

This work is done by checking `appState.mosaicArrangement`, which goes from an object to a single `EditorId` string when only one window is open.
commit_message: fix: ensure you have at least one editor open

pr_description:
Fixes #319.

This does two things to improve the Bisect experience, based on my own usage of the feature:
* Upon selecting "Good" or "Bad", we kill the current Fiddle app.
* The "Good" and "Bad" buttons are disabled while the current Electron version is downloading.

pr_description:
Ref #206

We've been wanting to move from TSLint to ESLint and Prettier for a while. The maintainers of TSLint deprecated the package last year (https://github.com/palantir/tslint/issues/4534), so this is a good time to transition.

I tried to adapt our previous TSLint config to match the new ESLint one as much as possible, but tried to keep it close to the default Prettier settings as well.

Note that this PR also adds `lint-staged` and `husky` for pre-commit hook linting.

Fiddle can't perform more than one action at a given time, so it makes more sense to place all potential actions in one state variable.

We also had some state variables in the Mobx store and some not, and as we should disable the command bar during _any_ action it makes the most sense to place this overarching state variable in the Mobx store.

cc @felixrieseberg

pr_description:
Closes https://github.com/electron/fiddle/issues/322

I added a state listener for when someone opens the theme selector. I could not figure out a way to stop the events from propagating and closing the settings besides this solution. It might be better to make this more generic of `prevenSettingsClose` or something, but I am not sure.

![ezgif com-optimize](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36998210/95143258-d3511e00-0732-11eb-9878-7d4c401182cb.gif)

I saw that this is a good first issue to test my OSS contributions on, I am mainly an Angular developer and have only dabbled a little in React so if there is a better way to make these changes, please let me know.

There are three places where `sortedElectronMap()` is used:

* `src/renderer/components/settings-electron.tsx` where its use appears to predate `appState.versionsToShow` because it is doing the exact same work. These extra steps can be removed by using `appState.versionsToShow` instead.

* `src/renderer/remote-loader.ts` which again looks like it's a post-refactor leftover. Its need is "has appState heard of version X?". It does this by sorting the array before calling `array.includes()` :slightly_smiling_face: These extra steps can be removed by using `appState.hasVersion()` instead.

* Last is `src/renderer/state.ts` which _does_ need to sort versions. However it does not need `sortedElectronMap()`'s transform step. It also doesn't need to pass an Object in as input -- it's simpler computationally to filter _before_ sorting, so this refactor does so. Happily, after removing the Object input and the transform step, what's left is a straightforward function that can be understood at-a-glance: `sortVersions(version: RunnableVersions[])`

*edit:* no new untested lines/branches. Coveralls is sad because this PR removes a few more lines from `src/` than it adds.

This PR revamps the version filters in File > Preferences > Electron:

1. The `statesToShow` toggles exist because those toggles fit the implementation, e.g. there's not really a use case for saying, e.g. "I only want to see versions that aren't downloaded." ... and the "Ready" toggle is unclickable with a tooltip that reads "Always enabled." This PR replaces that row with one toggle: "Show non-downloaded versions?"

2. The 'unsupported' filter has been removed. Back in 2019(?) the project made an Electron release tagged 'unsupported' because of reasons. It was a one-off thing that hasn't been done in years and doesn't need to be in the UI.

3. A new 'obsolete' filter has been added. This filter toggles whether or not end-of-lifed versions of Electron should be shown. This is a useful setting because disabling EOL versions by default (a) cuts down `versionsToShow` down from 580 versions to 111, and (b) signals to new users that it's preferable to write code on supported versions. Maintainers or users who need access to EOL versions can toggle the setting to get the same long list we know and love :heart: 

Removing `statesToShow` will make it possible to [add another state](https://github.com/electron/fiddle/issues/644) without complicating this UI :smile: 

## Before

![old-filters](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/70381/114499871-976ca900-9bec-11eb-8e1d-fcb5319d60f7.png)

## After

![new-filters](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/70381/114499894-a2273e00-9bec-11eb-961a-2403f68fe074.png)

## Discussion

This PR currently disables EOL versions for the reasons listed above but it is a change that could affect users. I'm open to feedback if anyone has reasons that end-of-lifed versions should be shown by default.
commit_message:
feat: simplify states to show in electron settings

In master, users can filter versions by VersionState. This introduces
some options without any use case, e.g. filtering based on whether or
not a version is currently being unzipped (an operation that takes less
than a second).

This change reduces those options down to the one that matters:
"Show undownloaded versions?"

Part 2 of breaking https://github.com/electron/fiddle/pull/664 into smaller, human-reviewable pieces.

This part removes the code for the Docs & Demos experiment that was started but not finished a year or so ago. Some of the development code could be tried out by manually setting a `FIDDLE_DOCS_DEMOS` environment variable but it didn't have any working features.

Side-effects:
1. The `PanelId` type goes away, since its only value was `PanelId.docsDemo`.
2. `MosaicId` goes away, since it was defined as `EditorId + PanelId` and is now redundant since it equals `EditorId`.
3. `ALL_PANELS` goes away, since it only held `PanelId.docsDemo`.
4. `ALL_MOSAICS` goes away, since it was defined as `DEFAULT_EDITORS + ALL_PANELS` and is now redundant since it equals `DEFAULT_EDITORS`.
5. `isEditorId()` and `isPanelId()` go away, since they were only used to differentiate between editors and the now-nonexistent panels.
6. `closedPanels` was defined as a `Record<Id, EditorBackup|true>` where `EditorBackup` was used for editors and `true` was used for panels. Now that panels are gone, `closedPanels` is now `Record<id, EditorBackup>`.
7. `isBackupEditor()` goes away since it was only used to test whether a value in `closedPanels` was an `EditorBackup` or `true`. Since this was the last remaining function in `type-checks`, that file and its spec also go away.
commit_message: refactor: remove unused Docs & Demos panels code

6. `closedPanels` was defined as a `Record<Id, EditorBackup|true>` where `EditorBackup` was used for editors and `true` was used for panels. Now that panels are gone, `closedPanels` is now `Record<id, EditorBackup>`.
7. `isBackupEditor()` goes away since it was only used to test whether a value in `closedPanels` was an `EditorBackup` or `true`. Since this was the last remaining function in `type-checks`, that file and its spec also go away.

7. `isBackupEditor()` goes away since it was only used to test whether a value in `closedPanels` was an `EditorBackup` or `true`. Since this was the last remaining function in `type-checks`, that file and its spec also go away.

Part 4 of breaking #664 into smaller, human-reviewable pieces.

This one is a very incremental change: replace `window.ElectronFiddle.editors` with `appState.editorMosaic`, which is a new class which just holds a `Map<EditorId, Monaco.editor.IStandaloneCodeEditor`. That's all! global -> non-global + Record -> Map. The important thing for future parts is that now there is a logical place to start consolidating mosaic code.

Structurally this is a _really_ simple PR. The diff is because snapshots feel every ripple in the mocks, and we've added a new class to the mocks. So don't be afraid of the diff size; this isn't 2K LOC being added :smile_cat:

Part 5 of breaking https://github.com/electron/fiddle/pull/664 into smaller, human-reviewable pieces.

Move mosaic logic from `AppState` and `App` into `EditorMosaic`. Also move `getEditorValue()`, `getEditorBackup()`, `getEditorViewState()`, `getEditorModel()`, and `updateEditorLayout()` from `utils/` into `EditorMosaic`.

No significant logic changes; this is just consolidation. E.g. the `EditorBackup` type is still public for testing even though the only production customer is EditorMosaic. The goal here is to move most of the related mosaic code into one place so that the responsibilities / dependencies are clearer before we start changing implementation.

![](https://imgflip.com/s/meme/Put-It-Somewhere-Else-Patrick.jpg)
commit_message: refactor: move AppState mosaic code to EditorMosaic

Co-authored-by: VerteDinde <vertedinde@electronjs.org>

Users can be confused when adding a local electron build using the same folder path as a previously added (but not removed) build.

current behaviour checks that the path has been added before and ignores it, without feedback to the user.

This fix aims to resolve any user confusion as to why their local electron build "did not upload"

- [x] added check for existing local version
- [x] added snapshot test to ensure dialog box shows when expected

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33054982/123000927-0856dc80-d365-11eb-917a-6f862efb4d51.png)
commit_message:
fix: add dialog box for when user adds duplicate local electron builds

Co-authored-by: VerteDinde <vertedinde@electronjs.org>

This PR mainly replaces the `commands-editor.tsx` component in the commands bar with a new sidebar next to the editors. I think this is an improvement since it aligns with other editors' UX, and makes the editor list visible with 0 clicks.

![Screenshot of change](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16010076/135187045-f6d34e26-4018-4dd2-937c-721370f195ec.png)

The only orthogonal change is a code deletion in ddfc727, which allows the user to have 0 editors open and exposes the non-ideal state when that happens:

![non-ideal state](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16010076/135187331-3eacbe92-3f8e-48d2-9962-d71f6f8672f6.png)

I think giving users more flexibility like this is more intuitive.


cc @codebytere
commit_message: feat: replace editors pane in command bar with sidebar

The only orthogonal change is a code deletion in ddfc727, which allows the user to have 0 editors open and exposes the non-ideal state when that happens:

![non-ideal state](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16010076/135187331-3eacbe92-3f8e-48d2-9962-d71f6f8672f6.png)

I think giving users more flexibility like this is more intuitive.


cc @codebytere

pr_description:
## Motivation

Our current way of including NPM registry packages is a bit hard to understand for users. It'd be better to be more explicit with which packages we're installing.

## Implementation

Removes the auto-magic NPM module parsing from Fiddle and replaces it with a pane in the sidebar. This initial implementation changes nothing with the underlying install logic, just how the modules are fetched (from the new `appState.modules` state variable).

The change also refactors the `src/utils/get-package.ts` utility and adds additional logic in `src/renderer/remote-loader.ts` so that the new sidebar package manager can handle importing and exporting gists.

## Further improvements

There are a lot of ways we can improve on this experience, including:

* Allowing users to select a specific version of the module they're installing
* Installing on select rather than on each run (ref https://github.com/electron/fiddle/issues/530)
* Auto-filling package information from the registry (e.g. by applying for the [algolia/npm-search](https://github.com/algolia/npm-search) program or querying an API like `npms.io`).


## Screenshot

![screenshot of fiddle with new package manager pane](https://github.com/electron/fiddle/blob/eb147585f55405684618bb7e49b2333f547209b5/assets/screenshots/fiddle-screenshot.png?raw=true)

pr_description:
ref https://github.com/electron/fiddle/issues/958

Adds autofill with Algolia's `npm-search` client. Allows the end-user to select versions from the package manager and get suggestions from the NPM registry.

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] Can add a package and run a Fiddle containing the package.
- [x] Defaults to the latest version of the package.
- [x] Can change versions between runs with a select input.
- [x] Has backward compatibility with older `package.json` formats (we used to pass each version as `*`).
- [x] Exporting the Fiddle to a Gist or local folder preserves package information.
- [x] Importing the Fiddle to a Gist or local folder preserves package information.
- [x] Loads the version list for imported gists
